title: Anthropic hits $965B as Opus 4.8 ships and Mythos goes wide - TCR 05/29/26
description:
Anthropic closed a Series H at $965 billion to surpass OpenAI as the world's most valuable AI company, shipped Claude Opus 4.8 with calibrated honesty and user-directed effort controls, and timetabled Mythos-class cyber-defensive access for all customers within weeks, while an MIT-led process commercialized by Rock Zero extracted battery-grade lithium plus smelter-grade alumina and cement-ready silica from spodumene at room temperature, and Scribe Therapeutics' single dose of STX-1150 held LDL cholesterol down more than 50% for 22 months in primates.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-29-2026/

0:00 What shipped
1:59 Anthropic's $965B valuation and Opus 4.8 honesty controls
4:49 Rock Zero's low-temperature hard-rock lithium process
7:37 AWS rebuilds search for agent traffic and the LLM futures market
10:15 Hyperscalers cross from power buyers to power builders
13:36 Blue Origin New Glenn lost on the pad
16:27 Close

This episode covers Anthropic's $965B valuation and Opus 4.8 honesty controls, the Mythos two-tier window timetabling its expiration, Rock Zero's low-temperature hard-rock lithium process, AWS rebuilding serverless infrastructure for agent traffic, derivatives exchanges racing to launch LLM token and GPU futures, hyperscalers crossing from power buyers to power builders, and Blue Origin's New Glenn lost on the Cape Canaveral pad during a static fire test.

The safety-first interpretability lab is now the most valuable AI company on Earth on the strength of a flagship model whose explicitly-marketed property is uncertainty-flagging rather than fluency, with revenue run-rate reaching $47 billion against $10 billion a year earlier. Mythos-class access, held under restricted release to forty-plus partner organizations since April, was timetabled this week to reach every customer within weeks - the two-tier release pattern that hardened across 2026 announcing its expiration date the same week the capital architecture realigned around the lab that built it. Yet-Ming Chiang's MIT team and the Rock Zero spinout opened a closed-loop, multi-product path to hard-rock lithium that recovers battery-grade metal, smelter-grade alumina, and cement-ready silica from the same ore at temperatures under 95 degrees Celsius, with projected costs below $6,000 per ton. AWS released a new generation of OpenSearch Serverless with scale-to-zero compute decoupled from storage, designed around the bursty traffic pattern of AI agents that did not exist eighteen months ago. The Shanghai Futures Exchange, CME Group, and Intercontinental Exchange are racing to launch contracts for LLM tokens and GPU compute hours. Hyperscalers facing the same three-year turbine and transformer lead times as utilities, but unconstrained by rate-recovery review, are building their own gigawatts directly. Scribe Therapeutics' STX-1150 reached Australian Phase 1 clearance after a single dose silenced PCSK9 transcription and held LDL down for nearly two years in primates without altering DNA.
